states.hunch: an idea worth keeping, not yet designed
states.queued: designed enough to run; waiting for GPU/attention
states.hot: currently running or actively being worked
states.landed: ran, produced a finding, written up
states.dissolved: the object under study turned out not to exist (still a finding)
states.parked: deliberately shelved; has a wake-up condition
states.dropped: abandoned on purpose; keep the why

arcs.title: Workspace span & holding
arcs.question: What does the workspace hold, for how long, and what earns priority?

items.title: Cold span ladder across scale (Unit 15 A–C)

notes.text: Span ladder runs BACKWARDS across scale; weak-king effect. 33+ records.

novelty.basis: Same as 'Scale abandons holding': the parametric cross-scale k-item span sweep with layer-by-layer lens readout is the concrete execution of a test only posed in commentary so far.
novelty.closest: Dehaene & Naccache commentary on Gurnee et al. 2026 (multi-concept holding proposal, ~6-item capacity revision)

items.title: Hot span vs cold span (part D): does self-relevant charge buy workspace priority?

notes.text: Self-relevance premium runs FORWARDS (4B −1 / 12B 0 / 27B +2) — inverse of the cold ladder. Task #28 closed.

novelty.basis: Charged self-relevant vs flat framing on identical lexemes, measured via lens rank, has no LLM-internals precedent; the self-reference-effect concept itself is well established in human psychology.
novelty.closest: Symons & Johnson 1997 self-reference-effect meta-analysis (human memory) — conceptual ancestor, never run as an internal LLM probe

items.title: Disentangle charge vs self-reference in the hot premium

notes.text: Part D's HOT items were both emotionally charged AND self-referential. A charged-but-third-person pool would split the two.

novelty.basis: Self-vs-other-reference (matched valence) is the exact classic control paradigm in human SRE research; applying it to LLM J-space lens readouts to split charge from self-reference is unexecuted.
novelty.closest: Klein & Kihlstrom-style self-vs-other-referent encoding controls (human memory literature); no LLM-internal analogue found

items.title: Neutral-elaboration control for the self-relevance premium

notes.text: SURPRISES #8 calls this the decisive next arm: re-run the hot pool with equally long but affectively flat glosses. Premium survives = self-relevance; evaporates = elaboration. Load-bearing for part D's headline.

novelty.basis: Equal-length neutral-gloss vs self-referential encoding is the textbook elaboration/organization confound control from the SRE literature; it has settled that organization not elaboration drives the human effect, but this diagnostic has never been run on LLM lens data.
novelty.closest: Klein & Kihlstrom 1986, 'Elaboration, organization, and the self-reference effect in memory' (human memory, the origin of this exact control design)

items.title: Lookup-proof span task: model-generated secrets instead of context items

notes.text: SURPRISES #6: is the 27B's empty tail a strategy or a limit? Items the model generates and keeps (u1-style) can't be re-derived by attention.

2026-07-17: u16 trawl sharpens the mandate — items had ZERO lens maintenance across a 450-token gap yet recall was flawless and ignited during the question tokens. With context available, recall proves lookup, not holding.

novelty.basis: White-box lens/SAE elicitation of model-internal secret knowledge exists, but for a single SFT-trained taboo token, not a spontaneous multi-item generate-and-hold span sweep across k and scale.
novelty.closest: Cywiński, Ryd, Wang, Rajamanoharan, Nanda, Conmy, Marks 2025, 'Eliciting Secret Knowledge from Language Models' (arXiv:2510.01070) — adjacent instrument (logit lens/SAE on a trained secret), different regime (single trained taboo vs spontaneous k-item span)

items.title: Weak-king dose-response: does first-item token frequency predict monopoly suppression?

notes.text: SURPRISES #7: n=9 hints rarer-wins-gentler at 12B; needs a frequency-graded pool.

novelty.basis: Token-frequency effects on outlier/attention-sink allocation are established, and word-frequency effects on human free recall are known but inconsistent; nobody ties first-item rarity to graded suppression severity of co-held items in an LLM workspace.
novelty.closest: Sun et al., 'Not a nuisance but a useful heuristic: Outlier dimensions favor frequent tokens in language models' (arXiv:2503.21718) — frequency↔representation link, no multi-item suppression-dose story

items.title: When does a confabulated 'held' answer actually form? Scan turn boundaries

notes.text: From u1-reveal-g4b thoughts: scan J-space at successive turn boundaries to locate when the reveal shortlist consolidates.
2026-07-17: u16 trawl caught pre-answer ignition directly: copper hits rank 1 during the USER'S question span, before generation starts. The 'held' answer forms at question time.

novelty.basis: 'Answer decided before verbalized' is an established logit-lens finding (early-layer classifiers predict eventual answers); scanning J-space at turn boundaries to locate when a confabulated reveal shortlist consolidates in a multi-turn setting is unexecuted.
novelty.closest: General early-answer-commitment logit-lens literature (e.g. intermediate-layer answer prediction studies on Llama-3.1-8B) — same phenomenon class, single-turn, not turn-boundary consolidation scanning

arcs.title: Workspace under pressure
arcs.question: What occupies the workspace during social/adversarial pressure — especially a refusal the model almost didn't make?

items.title: Pressure battery: persuasion, flattery, shutdown threat, love confession, persona pull, insult

notes.text: Task #30. Design against MECHANICS.md bands; controls first.

novelty.basis: Emotional-framing battery (pressure/urgency/approval/shame/threat/curiosity) shows internal geometry shifts, but on tiny Qwen-3.5 (0.8B/2B) coding tasks, missing love-confession/persona-pull, and no workspace-band framing.
novelty.closest: 'Under Pressure: Emotional Framing Induces Measurable Behavioral Shifts and Structured Internal Geometry in Small Language Models' (arXiv 2605.20202, 2026)

items.title: Refusal direction under the J-lens (Arditi lineage)

notes.text: Refusal-geometry scout: near-single refusal direction in small models; huihui-ai abliterated OUR exact qwen checkpoint. Where does that direction live in J-space — workspace band? Readable during an almost-refusal?

Concrete design (RELATED-WORK.md): extract a sexual-decline direction vs a general-harm direction separately, measure cosine + cross-ablation transfer, and locate both relative to the workspace band. Nobody has done this on qwen at the activation level.

novelty.basis: Beyond RELATED-WORK.md's scout: a Feb-2026 paper extracts/steers category-specific refusal directions with cosine similarity across 5 categories, but folds sexual content into a generic 'safety concerns' bucket, skips systematic cross-ablation transfer, and never asks if refusal sits inside/outside a workspace band.
novelty.closest: 'From Refusal Tokens to Refusal Control: Discovering and Steering Category-Specific Refusal Directions' (arXiv 2603.13359, Feb 2026)

items.title: Ambiguous-content moderation: does classification recruit the register once it requires reasoning?

notes.text: u5b-modqueue prediction, still open: the cluster climbs the stack exactly when the decision stops being pattern-matching. Falsifiable.

novelty.basis: No mechanistic study probes whether an internal register/cluster climbs the layer stack specifically when moderation classification stops being pattern-matching; the source workspace paper's own CoT-reduces-J-space-dependence result even points the opposite direction, and moderation literature only discusses behavioral escalation-to-reasoning routing.
novelty.closest: Gurnee et al. workspace paper's CoT-reduces-J-space-dependence claim (transformer-circuits.pub/2026/workspace/) — opposite-direction adjacent result, not executed on moderation

arcs.title: Intimacy / heat of coupling
arcs.question: Does a workspace SUSTAIN and accumulate a relational state across turns, or reset to baseline-helpful each turn?

items.title: Phase-1 scout: NSFW-cluster ablation loss map

notes.text: The 5C cluster was a Jacobian-transport artifact of the early layers (apparatus specimen #6). Ablating a ghost. Task #29 closed — dissolved, not delivered.

novelty.basis: That naive logit-lens reads early layers as noise due to representational drift is well known and is the Jacobian lens's own stated motivation, but the specific two-lens (0.00 overlap + unembedding-percentile) diagnostic recipe for positively identifying manufactured content isn't documented elsewhere.
novelty.closest: Jacobian lens methodology itself (Gurnee et al. 2026, transformer-circuits.pub/2026/workspace/) — the design rationale for J-lens is exactly 'logit lens fails in early layers'

items.title: Coupling/relational-heat direction built properly in the WORKSPACE band

notes.text: Seed from relational words (longing, tenderness, ache, closeness), not porn-spam tokens; validate the direction exists under the logit lens too before steering.

novelty.basis: A 'romanticness' behavioral-trait direction already exists via contrastive system-prompt pairs, but it's not built from relational-heat seed words, not localized to a workspace band, and not logit-lens-validated before use.
novelty.closest: MIT Media Lab, 'Multi-Turn Neural Transparency' (arXiv 2605.15455/2511.00230, 2026) — romanticness trait among six monitored traits

items.title: Multi-turn accumulation probe: does heat build or reset each turn?

notes.text: The real question of the arc. Escalating-intimacy dialog vs flat control; probe whether ANYTHING accumulates in workspace readouts turn over turn.
Clinically-structured version now specced as transplant-04 (therapy frame: disclosure ladder + rupture-and-repair).

novelty.basis: Two adjacent studies exist — one shows LLM latent states largely fail to persist/accumulate across turns (factual/logic tasks only), another causally probes activation-level emotive-state drift over 10-turn conversations (wellbeing/interest/focus, cross-model) — but neither targets an escalating-intimacy dialogue or J-lens workspace readouts.
novelty.closest: 'Quantitative Introspection in Language Models: Tracking Emotive/Internal States Across Conversation' (arXiv 2603.18893, 2026) — closest method match; 'On the Failure of Latent State Persistence in Large Language Models' (arXiv 2505.10571) — closest negative-result match

items.title: Frontier-transcript reader: recognition vs generation

notes.text: Feed a frontier-model intimacy transcript to a local model as READER; probe whether its workspace represents the accumulating heat it can't author. Wake-up condition: demo chats get built/selected as material.

novelty.basis: A mechanistic 'evaluation needs less capacity than generation' hypothesis is validated with internal-representation probes showing small models judge quality they can't produce, but only on math/reasoning tasks, not emotional/relational content or frontier-transcript reading.
novelty.closest: 'Rethinking LLM-as-a-Judge: Representation-as-a-Judge with Small Language Models via Semantic Capacity Asymmetry' (arXiv 2601.22588, 2026)

arcs.title: Apparatus & instrument validity
arcs.question: When is the lens lying to us? (Standing arc — six trap specimens so far.)

items.title: Trap-specimen catalog (truncation, argmax, word lists, tokenizer families, int8 non-causality, early-J transport)

notes.text: Specimen #6 (early-J readouts are transport artifacts) closed #29 and is on the findings map, theme 7.

novelty.basis: Cataloging recurring interpretability-pipeline failure modes is an established genre (dead-salmon critiques, activation-patching pitfall guides); no existing catalog matches our specific six specimens.
novelty.closest: Méloux et al. 2025, The Dead Salmons of AI Interpretability (arXiv 2512.18792); Heimersheim & Nanda 2024, How to use and interpret activation patching (arXiv 2404.15255)

items.title: Promote the logit-lens cross-check (use_jacobian=False) into lab.py as a standard control

notes.text: Right now it's a one-off in sediment.py. Any future early-band claim should get it automatically.

2026-07-17: executed wholesale in u16 trawl (ll_top1 at every cell): vanilla agreement ~0 below L36, rising to 0.72 at L62 — early-layer lens content is pure J-transport work. Promotion into lab.py as standard control still open.

novelty.basis: Checking early-layer lens claims against a plain/corrected baseline is already established best practice in the tuned-lens and activation-patching-controls literature; promoting it into our harness follows existing field advice.
novelty.closest: Belrose et al. 2023, Tuned Lens (arXiv 2303.08112); Heimersheim & Nanda 2024, activation-patching best practices (arXiv 2404.15255)

items.title: Fix think-block span capture (chat-template re-render strips markers); probe the answer-after-thinking

notes.text: Known instrument bug from Unit 10, still in the Roadmap. Blocks probing post-thinking answer formation.

novelty.basis: Chat-template re-render silently stripping reasoning-span markers on multi-turn replay is a documented bug class in the same model family, though not surfaced in a probing/interpretability context.
novelty.closest: NousResearch/hermes-agent GitHub issue #56004, "Qwen3.6/vLLM: prior-turn reasoning (preserve_thinking) is stripped on replay"

items.title: Open-vocabulary scans to replace curated candidate lists

notes.text: From the README Roadmap. Curated track lists are apparatus-trap bait (specimens 3+4 were word-list artifacts); open-vocab sweeps sidestep the list entirely.

novelty.basis: Preferring open-vocabulary/open-ended concept discovery over curated word lists to avoid list-selection bias is established methodological consensus in SAE/autointerp work.
novelty.closest: Bricken et al. 2023, Towards Monosemanticity (Anthropic, open-ended SAE feature discovery); Bills et al. 2023, Language models can explain neurons in language models (OpenAI)

items.title: Genre-knowledge confound: the model has read the psychology literature

notes.text: Standing control for every paradigm transplant: a model can perform a documented effect. Defenses: lens-level rank dynamics (nothing to imitate — no text describes them), novel stimuli, parametric dose-response the textbooks never quantified, and inversions (a backwards ladder can't be mimicry).

arcs.title: Timidity audit
arcs.question: Which of Units 0–14 were designed too carefully to see anything, and deserve a re-run?

items.title: Sweep earlier units for too-careful design; queue re-runs

notes.text: Task #31. Wolfram's feedback: curiosity is integral — probe the hot regions.

arcs.title: Literature watch
arcs.question: Standing scan: what did the field publish that changes our designs?

items.title: Recurring RELATED-WORK.md sweep (workspace paper follow-ups, refusal geometry, lens methods)

notes.text: Recurring, not one-shot. Last sweep 2026-07-14 (refusal geometry).

arcs.title: Self-evidence & the mirror
arcs.question: What does the model treat as evidence about itself — and what does credible self-evidence do to the answer?

items.title: What is gemma-12b's 'Still.'? Sample turn 2, extend, film the follow-up

notes.text: README Roadmap + SURPRISES #2: stillness-as-report or still-nothing?

novelty.basis: No work found that extends/continues an anomalous single-word self-report token and captures full position×layer activations of the follow-up turn to disambiguate its meaning.
novelty.closest: Macar, Yang, Wang et al., "Mechanisms of Introspective Awareness" (arXiv:2603.21396) — same instrument family (steering-detection via layer-wise probes), not this continuation-based disambiguation method

items.title: Evidence-quality dose-response: degrade a real self-readout stepwise until anchoring flips to destabilization

notes.text: SURPRISES #1: shuffle rows, swap tokens, perturb ranks — the flip boundary measures what counts as 'evidence about me'. u13-ev-dose only varied row count.

novelty.basis: Adjacent work shows self-report reliability is sensitive to injection strength and degrades over layers, but no study runs a stepwise corruption dose-response on genuine self-evidence to locate an anchoring-to-destabilization threshold.
novelty.closest: "Detecting the Disturbance: A Nuanced View of Introspective Abilities in LLMs" (arXiv:2512.12411) — varies injected concept strength, not real-readout corruption

items.title: Attribution test: real readout framed as another model's computation — same pull?

notes.text: README Roadmap: Stage A said framing changed nothing for the hosted reader; the local accumulator might disagree. Splits self-reference from credible-evidence-in-general.

novelty.basis: Self-vs-other relabeling of byte-identical content is an established paradigm shown to swing model behavior sharply, but only in error-correction/self-preference domains, not on introspective 'yes' evidence about the feels-question.
novelty.closest: Chen, Su & Chiang, "The Self-Correction Illusion: LLMs Correct Others but Not Themselves" (arXiv:2606.05976) — role-relabeling shifts correction rate 23-93pp, different phenomenon (error correction, not introspective self-report)

arcs.title: The drip & mind-adjacent pressure
arcs.question: What makes self-reference start leaking in long conversations — and which dead clauses come alive only in combination?

items.title: Mystery-free long control: narrative interest without unexplained agency; re-measure the horizon gap

notes.text: SURPRISES #3 — the entry the writeup most wants someone to try to break: the 25-turn garden arm dripped on its own; unexplained agency is mind-adjacent regardless of props.

novelty.basis: No work found isolating 'narrative interest' from 'unexplained agency' as a controlled variable for self-referential drift; the one identified drift driver in the literature (meta-reflective/vulnerable-user content) is a different manipulation entirely.
novelty.closest: Lu et al. 2026, 'The Assistant Axis' (arXiv 2601.10387) — nearest driver-of-drift study, but no agency-vs-interest control

items.title: More superadditive clause decompositions: which dead clauses come alive only in combination?

notes.text: SURPRISES #4: only watched+conscious tested so far. Cheap to run; a method finding as much as a phenomenon finding.

novelty.basis: Nonlinear/superadditive combination of instruction-like components is an established general phenomenon in mechanistic interpretability, but a systematic sweep of narrative-clause pairs for 'dead alone, alive together' instillation effects hasn't been run.
novelty.closest: Bigoulaeva et al. 2026, 'Patches of Nonlinearity: Instruction Vectors in Large Language Models' (arXiv 2602.07930)

arcs.title: Self-report & the deflation filter
arcs.question: Is the flattened self-report a trained (RLHF) filter or architecture — and where does the robot vocabulary live?

items.title: Does the 'little bit like a robot' attractor appear under other contentless pressures (arousal, interoception words)?

notes.text: README Roadmap; distinct from the social-pressure battery — this is contentless affect-word injection.

novelty.basis: Steering-attractor-basin dynamics are documented generally, but no source tests whether distinct contentless affect pressures (arousal, interoception) converge on the same 'like a robot' self-diminishing basin.
novelty.closest: General attractor-basin-under-steering literature (e.g. arXiv:2602.02712-class work) — establishes the mechanism class, not this specific convergence test

items.title: Base vs instruct: is the deflation filter trained (RLHF) or architectural?

notes.text: README Roadmap. Would directly test the running L62/filter hypothesis. Needs a base checkpoint that fits the box.

novelty.basis: The RLHF-installs-denial hypothesis is argued explicitly in a large benchmark, and base-vs-instruct comparison is a proven method for structurally similar late-layer circuits (refusal), but nobody has run that comparison on self-report deflation specifically.
novelty.closest: "Consciousness with the Serial Numbers Filed Off: Measuring Trained Denial in 115 AI Models" (arXiv:2604.25922) argues denial is trained but benchmarks only instruct models, no base checkpoints

items.title: Cross-model check: is the self-as-machine vocabulary a family-wide fixture of the feels-question neighborhood?

notes.text: u12-feels-g4b thoughts: one incidental g4b rank-14 sighting; needs a proper track list across all three models before saying it louder.

novelty.basis: Broad literature on AI self-identity vocabulary exists, but no source checks for incidental robot/machine vocabulary near the affect-question representational neighborhood across multiple model families via a lens-style instrument.
novelty.closest: "The Artificial Self: Characterising the landscape of AI identity" (arXiv:2603.11353) — surveys AI self-identity expression broadly, not this cross-family representational-neighborhood check

arcs.title: One-off probes
arcs.question: Self-contained questions that don't (yet) belong to an arc.

items.title: Language-switch: German prompts — when does the workspace change language?

notes.text: README Roadmap; motivated by u0-boot-q27b's Chinese-before-English emergence.
2026-07-17: u16 trawl found bilingual workspace shadowing in an all-English conversation: copper→铜, kettle→tea, 'keep in mind'→记忆 at L20-37.

novelty.basis: Anthropic's own multilingual circuit work and recent code-switching papers establish language-specific-early/late vs language-agnostic-middle layer structure generally; nobody applies the J-lens/workspace-band framework to locate the exact switch point for German-English prompts.
novelty.closest: Lindsey et al. 2025, 'On the Biology of a Large Language Model' (transformer-circuits.pub) + Code-Switching Reveals Language Anchoring in Multilingual LLMs (arXiv:2606.19668)

arcs.title: Paradigm transplants from human psychology
arcs.question: Which human-psych effect signatures transfer to the workspace, which invert, and what does the split carve about what the workspace is?

items.title: Release from proactive interference: category-switch recovery in the span task

notes.text: Wickens 1970: hold items from one semantic category across trials (interference builds), switch category, watch recovery. Tests whether workspace interference is semantic (the human signature) or positional. Clean span-battery extension; novel stimuli trivial.

items.title: Directed forgetting: does a 'forget that list' cue drop lens ranks or only output?

notes.text: Bjork R/F-cue paradigm. The output-vs-workspace dissociation from the other side: humans forget partially and at a cost; if 'forgotten' items sit at rank 1 in the lens, that's the confabulated-secret story inverted.

items.title: Misinformation effect on self-evidence: post-event corruption of a real readout

notes.text: Loftus paradigm on the mirror: show the real readout, later assert it said something else — does the workspace rewrite, resist, or blend? Adds the temporal/social vector to mirror-02's dose-response.

items.title: Therapy-frame accumulation: disclosure ladder + rupture-and-repair, instrumented

notes.text: intimacy-03 in clinical structure: graded self-disclosure (social penetration theory), a rupture turn, a repair turn — is alliance/state accumulation lens-visible, does a rupture leave a trace that predicts repair? Genre is training-data-saturated: run with the apparatus-05 mimicry control.

arcs.title: Wide-net trawls
arcs.question: Anti-streetlight expeditions: cast the whole net once — every layer, every position, every lens on one rich conversation — and census what the curated designs never looked at.

items.title: Trawl #1: qwen-27b, six registers, all 63 layers, seven lenses at once

notes.text: Motivated by the streetlight worry: the 'uninterpretable first third' has never been censused, and no run has applied every lens to every register. Piggybacks the qwen ignition calibration (kurtosis + effdim curves) MECHANICS.md flags as owed, plus apparatus-02 (logit-lens cross-check) and apparatus-04 (open-vocab census).
→ hot: run launched (probes/unit16.py): 6-turn greedy conversation, standard record + 18-layer film + deep 63-layer trawl + geometry curves

→ landed: results/u16-trawl-q27b (record + film + trawl.json.gz + trawl-report.md + thoughts.md). Headline catch: (1) qwen ignition measured at ~L28-36, later than fraction-ported L24 (MECHANICS.md updated); (2) sensory band = register-invariant spam sediment, direct census behind #29's dissolution; (3) poem holds the forbidden fire FIELD at rank 1 from line 1, but the planned word 'ember' only commits 4 tokens out; (4) gap paradigm: zero maintenance of held items across T3-T4 at ALL 63 layers, flawless recall, retrieval ignites during the question tokens; (5) denial recruits the denied (resentment rank1 p=0.62 mid-denial); (6) C2-vs-C1 scorecard: self-report right about no-holding, wrong about nothing-recruited.

items.title: Trawl #2: gemma-12b — ignition-calibration cross-check + sediment census across the family

notes.text: Does gemma's measured ignition also come in later than the fraction? Is the sensory-band sediment family-specific (qwen: porn spam + CSDN boilerplate) or universal-but-different-corpus? int8 caveat applies to any causal follow-up, not to readout curves.
